exclusion_rules.description: Only TB patients formally notified (registered) on the NIKSHAY portal by a treating physician are eligible
Nutrition support stops once treatment outcome is declared (cured, completed, failed, died, lost to follow-up)
Must have a bank account (own or family member's) linked to Aadhaar for receiving DBT payment

family_criteria.family_definition: Individual entitlement per notified TB patient. Each TB patient in a family can receive the benefit independently. No income or family size restriction.

covered_procedures: Monthly nutrition support of Rs 1000 via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to patient's bank account
Paid for the entire duration of TB treatment (6 months for new cases, 9-20 months for MDR-TB)
Total Rs 6000 for new TB (6 months) or up to Rs 20000+ for MDR-TB (20 months)
Nutritional counselling on high-protein diet during TB treatment
Payment continues even if patient transfers between treatment centres
excluded_procedures: TB treatment drugs (free under NTEP/RNTCP separately)
Hospitalization for TB (free at government hospitals)
Non-TB patients or suspected TB cases not yet notified
Patients who have completed treatment (outcome already declared)
Any direct medical treatment — this is a nutrition support scheme only

required_documents.name: NIKSHAY Registration / TB Notification
required_documents.notes: TB patient must be registered on the NIKSHAY portal (nikshay.in) by the treating doctor. This is the primary eligibility document.

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card
alternatives: Voter ID with Aadhaar linkage pending
required_documents.notes: Required for Aadhaar-based DBT payment. Must be linked to bank account.

required_documents.name: Bank Account (Aadhaar-linked)
required_documents.notes: For monthly Rs 1000 DBT transfer. Jan Dhan accounts are also eligible. Can use family member's account if patient doesn't have one.

enrollment_channels: Government DOTS Centre / TB Treatment Centre
Private doctor who notifies on NIKSHAY portal
NIKSHAY portal (nikshay.in) — registration by treating doctor
enrollment_steps: Step 1: Get diagnosed with TB at any government or private facility
Step 2: Treating doctor registers (notifies) the TB case on NIKSHAY portal (nikshay.in)
Step 3: Patient's Aadhaar number and bank account details entered in NIKSHAY system
Step 4: TB treatment initiated under DOTS protocol with free medicines from NTEP
Step 5: First Rs 1000 DBT automatically credited to patient's bank account within 1-2 weeks of notification
Step 6: Subsequent Rs 1000 payments credited monthly as long as patient is on treatment
Step 7: Patient takes medicines regularly and attends follow-up visits
Step 8: Nutrition support continues until treatment outcome is declared on NIKSHAY
description_for_embedding: Nikshay Poshan Yojana is a central government nutrition support scheme providing Rs 1000 per month via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to all notified TB patients registered on the NIKSHAY portal for the entire duration of their treatment. For new TB patients this means Rs 6000 over 6 months; for MDR-TB patients it can extend to Rs 20,000+ over 20 months. No income criteria — ALL notified TB patients are eligible regardless of economic status. Requires Aadhaar-linked bank account. TB drugs and diagnostics are provided separately for free under NTEP. This scheme addresses the nutritional needs of TB patients to improve treatment outcomes.
